scope: S4096 layer-2 actual-LI compressed dKV producer/reducer branch for token/Q-dO-resident group-aware epilogue

feasibility.read: The corrected head-aware LDS model rejects the highest modeled-write-reduction token_window=32/64 rows for a Q/dO-resident producer. The first feasible high-return row is token_window=16 with selected/group panel 512 and micro_selected 256 under a 160 KiB budget; token_window=8 fits the stricter 96 KiB target but has lower write reduction.

rejected_branches.name: triton_full_active_table_u1024
rejected_branches.read: A one-CTA all-active-group accumulator for 16x256 is too large in Triton.

rejected_branches.name: triton_partial_active_table_u256
rejected_branches.read: Fits by splitting active groups into four active blocks, but reloads/recomputes Q/dO and performs dense route-table work per block. This destroys the fast query-major producer.

rejected_branches.name: tilelang_block_k128
rejected_branches.result: compiled but HIP launch failed with invalid argument
rejected_branches.read: Larger K sweep is not usable in the current TileLang selected-limit kernel shape.

rejected_branches.name: tilelang_block_d32
rejected_branches.read: Lower D-block LDS pressure loses because it doubles D-block work.

rejected_branches.name: tilelang_group_panel256
rejected_branches.read: Smaller group accumulator loses to extra panel work.

rejected_branches.name: tilelang_threads256
rejected_branches.read: Fewer threads lose versus the retained 512-thread kernel.

retained_candidates.name: tilelang_tb8_gp512_k64d64_thr512
retained_candidates.read: Best measured kernel-only/pack-plus-kernel timing in this sweep. Lower write reduction than TB32, but better scheduling.

retained_candidates.name: tilelang_tb32_gp512_k64d64_thr512
retained_candidates.read: Essentially tied with TB8 on kernel timing while preserving much higher modeled write reduction. Worth keeping as the integration candidate if route-plan stability matters more than the tiny timing edge.

retained_candidates.name: tilelang_tb16_gp512_k64d64_thr512_prior
retained_candidates.read: Stable prior base; slightly slower than TB8/TB32.

decision.why_faster_than_raw_sparse_atomic: It keeps the selected-limit TileLang MFMA producer and reduces by compressed group inside the CTA before global dKV writes, cutting global write/atomic volume versus one selected-row atomic per token/slot/D.
decision.why_not_e2e_yet: The fast kernel-only path still consumes materialized packed probabilities and scaled grad logits. The pack bridge costs about 2 ms and, in TorchTitan, earlier bridge paths also materialized selected-state tensors before TileLang. Production must fuse probability/dP production from q, dO, output/lse, compressed KV, and LI indices directly into this selected-limit body.
decision.next_step: Port/fuse the sparse-MLA backward boundary into the retained TileLang selected-limit body so it consumes q, dO, compressed KV, top indices/source indices, output, lse, and delta directly, with no global probs/grad_logits/selected-KV slabs.
